name: Faros AI Engineering Intelligence API
what_it_does: Open-source engineering intelligence platform with GraphQL API — enabling engineering teams to unify tooling data (GitHub, Jira, PagerDuty, CircleCI, etc.) into a semantic graph, calculate DORA metrics, build custom engineering dashboards, and run analytics queries via Hasura GraphQL engine, with community edition available for self-hosting.
use_cases: Unifying engineering tool data via Faros AI API for single-pane-of-glass analytics
Tracking DORA metrics via Faros AI for cross-tool engineering performance measurement
Building custom engineering dashboards via Faros AI GraphQL for bespoke analytics
Integrating CI/CD, Git, and issue tracker data via Faros AI for end-to-end visibility
Running engineering analytics queries via Faros AI for data-driven planning and reporting
not_for: Teams wanting fully managed SaaS without infrastructure management (community edition is self-hosted)
Simple DORA metrics without multi-tool integration complexity
Real-time operational monitoring (Faros is analytics/trends, not alerting)
best_when: An engineering team with diverse tooling (GitHub + Jira + PagerDuty + CircleCI) needs a unified data model and custom analytics — especially if they want open-source flexibility and GraphQL-native querying.
avoid_when: You need simple out-of-the-box DORA dashboards without infrastructure management, or prefer opinionated SaaS over flexible data platform.

auth.notes: API key via x-hasura-admin-secret header for Hasura GraphQL endpoint. Community edition: self-hosted Hasura + Postgres. Cloud edition: managed Faros AI cloud. GraphQL endpoint: configurable (self-hosted URL or Faros cloud). Faros CLI for data ingestion. REST API for data mutations (events ingestion). Multiple connector integrations require separate OAuth/API keys per tool (GitHub, Jira, PagerDuty, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community edition: free self-hosted; limited connectors
pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2020 by Farid Zakaria and Brett Loverro (ex-Google, ex-LinkedIn). $30M+ funding (Salesforce Ventures, Cowboy Ventures). Open-source community edition on GitHub (Apache 2.0). Architecture: Hasura GraphQL engine + Postgres for data storage + connectors for tool integration. Semantic data model: 'Faros graph' — unified engineering graph connecting code, issues, deployments, incidents. Connectors: GitHub, GitLab, Jira, PagerDuty, CircleCI, GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, Buildkite, Linear, Harness, ServiceNow, OpsGenie, VictorOps. Metabase integration for dashboards. DORA metrics out-of-the-box. Custom metrics via GraphQL. Used by: Box, Zendesk, Cloudflare.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL via Hasura (not REST). x-hasura-admin-secret header. Self-hosted or cloud. Open-source community edition. Multiple connector OAuth keys required. Faros CLI for ingestion. US company. SOC2/GDPR.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GraphQL queries idempotent. Data ingestion via events API — idempotent by event ID. Hasura mutations support upsert semantics.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL only (no REST API for queries) — Hasura GraphQL endpoint via x-hasura-admin-secret
Community edition requires self-hosting Hasura + Postgres — significant infrastructure setup
Multiple OAuth integrations required per tool (GitHub, Jira, PagerDuty each need separate credentials)
Faros CLI used for data ingestion — REST events API for programmatic ingestion
Data model complexity: Faros graph semantic model — learn the entity types before querying
Self-hosted community edition: no SLA — production reliability depends on your infrastructure

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Hasura admin secret — powerful credential; rotate regularly. Self-hosted community edition: security is operator's responsibility. Cloud edition: SOC2 certified. Multiple integration credentials (GitHub, Jira, PagerDuty) must all be secured. Engineering analytics data is sensitive leadership IP. Open-source codebase enables security auditing. Hasura: disable introspection in production.
